mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in the Consumer Price Index (CPI) over 6 months signals rising demand and inflation expectations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite positive signal.

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% decrease in the Unemployment Rate (UNRATE) over 3 months signals improving labor market conditions, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in improving labor market conditions are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in Industrial Production (INDPRO) over 6 months signals rising supply and economic growth, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite positive signal.

mechanism_narrative: A 1% increase in the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) over 3 months signals tightening monetary conditions, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1% decrease in the Consumer Price Index (CPI) over 6 months signals declining demand and deflation expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% increase in the Unemployment Rate (UNRATE) over 3 months signals deteriorating labor market conditions, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in deteriorating labor market conditions are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1% increase in Housing Starts (HOUST) over 6 months signals rising demand and economic growth, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days, but only when the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) is below 5%.
candidate_trigger: HOUST > 1% increase in 6 months and FEDFUNDS < 5%
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite positive signal.
